title: Sequoia's Roelof Botha: Why Venture Capital is Broken & How Great Companies Are Built
description:
(0:00) Introducing Roelof Botha

(1:08) Sequoia's Scout program and its best-performing funds ever

(3:38) State of Venture Capital: Why it's broken, too much capital, not enough great companies

(9:01) Why Sequoia separated from its China business, how they adapt to changing macro landscapes

(13:05) Sequoia's culture, picking partners, investment decision-making, The Sequoia Capital Fund and holding winners

(20:18) What makes a great founder, what Roelof learned from Doug Leone and Michael Moritz

(26:06) Investing in biotech, importance of expertise in VC

Thanks to our partners for making this happen!

Solana - Solana is the high performance network powering internet capital markets, payments, and crypto applications. Connect with investors, crypto founders, and entrepreneurs at Solana's global flagship event during Abu Dhabi Finance Week & F1: https://solana.com/breakpoint

IREN - IREN AI Cloud, powered by NVIDIA GPUs, provides the scale, performance, and reliability to accelerate your AI journey. https://iren.com/

Oracle - Step into the future of enterprise productivity at Oracle AI Experience Live. https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/data-ai-events/

BVNK - Building stablecoin-powered financial infrastructure that helps businesses send, store, and spend value instantly, anywhere in the world. https://www.bvnk.com/

Polymarket - The world's largest prediction market. https://www.polymarket.com/

analysis.summary: This episode of the All-In Podcast features Roelof Botha, a partner at Sequoia Capital, discussing the state of venture capital, what makes a great founder, and how Sequoia adapts to changing macro landscapes. Roelof also shares his insights on investing in biotech and the importance of expertise in VC. The conversation covers Sequoia's culture, investment decision-making, and the Scout program.

takeaways.text: Venture capital is broken due to too much capital and not enough great companies
Sequoia separated from its China business to adapt to changing macro landscapes
Great founders have a unique combination of skills and traits
Expertise is crucial in VC, especially in biotech investments
Sequoia's Scout program has produced some of the firm's best-performing funds
Holding winners is a key aspect of Sequoia's investment strategy
Sequoia's culture emphasizes picking the right partners and making informed investment decisions
Roelof learned valuable lessons from Doug Leone and Michael Moritz

sentiment.notes: The conversation has a cautious tone, with Roelof discussing the challenges in venture capital and the importance of adapting to changing macro landscapes.

notable_moments.description: Roelof discusses Sequoia's decision to separate from its China business
Roelof shares his insights on what makes a great founder
